description: Fix three minor but genuine issues in the MCP server: a dangling JSDoc comment, a hot-reload-unsafe module-level flag, and an O(n*m) allocation in a filter loop.

userStories.title: Fix dangling JSDoc comment opener in loader.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want loader.ts to have clean, well-formed comments so that JSDoc tooling and code review are not confused by a stray `/**` block opener.
acceptanceCriteria: The duplicate `/**` on line 67 of platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts is removed, leaving only the single JSDoc comment block for the `pluginNameFromPackage` function
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts, line 67 is `/**` followed immediately by line 68 which is another `/**`. The first `/**` is a stray comment opener — it should be removed entirely. The resulting file should have a single JSDoc block starting at what is currently line 68: `/** ... Extract the internal plugin name from an npm package name. ...`. This is a one-line deletion.

userStories.title: Move audit-disk initialized flag to globalThis for hot-reload safety
userStories.description: As a developer running the MCP server in dev mode, I want the audit-disk module's `initialized` flag to survive bun --hot reloads so that safeChmod is not called redundantly on every hot reload.
acceptanceCriteria: The `let initialized = false;` module-level variable in platform/mcp-server/src/audit-disk.ts is replaced with a globalThis-based pattern matching the convention used in log-buffer.ts, resolver.ts, and reload.ts
safeChmod is called at most once per server process lifetime, not once per hot reload
The _resetInitialized test helper still works (resets the globalThis key)
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/audit-disk.ts, line 22 has `let initialized = false;`. Under bun --hot, module-level variables reset on each re-evaluation, so this flag resets to false on every hot reload, causing a redundant safeChmod call on the next audit write. The fix is to store the flag on globalThis using a unique key (e.g., `__opentabs_audit_initialized__`), following the exact same pattern as `__opentabs_log_buffers__` in log-buffer.ts (line 37) and `__opentabs_global_paths__` in resolver.ts (line 137). The `_resetInitialized` export (used by tests) should reset the globalThis key to `false` instead of the module variable.

userStories.title: Eliminate repeated Array.from() allocation in pruneStaleState
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the outdatedPlugins pruning in reload.ts to use O(n+m) complexity instead of O(n*m) by pre-building a Set of npm package names.
acceptanceCriteria: The `state.outdatedPlugins.filter(...)` call in pruneStaleState (platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts, around line 96) no longer calls `Array.from(state.registry.plugins.values()).some(...)` inside the filter callback
A Set of npm package names is built once before the filter, and the filter uses Set.has() for O(1) lookup
Behavior is identical: only outdatedPlugins entries whose npmPackageName matches a current plugin are retained
userStories.notes:
In platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts, lines 96-98 have:
```
state.outdatedPlugins = state.outdatedPlugins.filter(o =>
  Array.from(state.registry.plugins.values()).some(p => p.npmPackageName === o.name),
);
```
This creates a new Array from the plugins Map on every iteration of the filter callback. Fix: before the filter, build `const npmPkgNames = new Set(Array.from(state.registry.plugins.values()).map(p => p.npmPackageName).filter((n): n is string => n !== undefined));` then use `state.outdatedPlugins = state.outdatedPlugins.filter(o => npmPkgNames.has(o.name));`. This is the final story and must have e2eCheckpoint: true.
